changes.value:
## What
Elicitation flows through MCP (`rmcp` crate), not ACP. The `swissarmyhammer-tools` crate already calls `peer.create_elicitation()` for MCP elicitation. The hooks should fire from the MCP proxy layer when elicitation requests/responses pass through.

Investigate where `create_elicitation` is called in:
- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/questions/ask/mod.rs`
- `model-context-protocol-extras/` (if there's a proxy/middleware layer)

Wire `HookEvent::Elicitation` before the elicitation request is sent, and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` after the user responds. The Elicitation hook should be able to deny the request (prevent the elicitation from reaching the user).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Elicitation hook fires when MCP server calls `create_elicitation`
- [ ] ElicitationResult hook fires when user responds
- [ ] Elicitation hook can deny the request
- [ ] Matcher filters by MCP server name

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: construct and fire Elicitation event, verify hook runs
- [ ] Test matcher filtering on mcp_server_name
- [ ] Run relevant crate tests — all pass
Wire Elicitation/ElicitationResult hooks into MCP proxy layer

+## BLOCKED\n\nThe MCP proxy layer (`model-context-protocol-extras/src/proxy.rs`) has no elicitation awareness. The `rmcp` crate's `create_elicitation` is an external API call that doesn't pass through any hookable middleware. Wiring this requires adding elicitation interception to the MCP proxy layer first.\n\n## Original Description\n\nElicitation flows through MCP (`rmcp` crate), not ACP. +## What
+Elicitation flows through MCP (`rmcp` crate), not ACP. The `swissarmyhammer-tools` crate already calls `peer.create_elicitation()` for MCP elicitation. The hooks should fire from the MCP proxy layer when elicitation requests/responses pass through.
+
+Investigate where `create_elicitation` is called in:
+- `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/questions/ask/mod.rs`
+- `model-context-protocol-extras/` (if there's a proxy/middleware layer)
+
+Wire `HookEvent::Elicitation` before the elicitation request is sent, and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` after the user responds. The Elicitation hook should be able to deny the request (prevent the elicitation from reaching the user).
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Elicitation hook fires when MCP server calls `create_elicitation`
+- [ ] ElicitationResult hook fires when user responds
+- [ ] Elicitation hook can deny the request
+- [ ] Matcher filters by MCP server name
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Unit test: construct and fire Elicitation event, verify hook runs
+- [ ] Test matcher filtering on mcp_server_name
+- [ ] Run relevant crate tests — all pass
\ No newline at end of file

changes.forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-## BLOCKED

The MCP proxy layer (`model-context-protocol-extras/src/proxy.rs`) has no elicitation awareness. The `rmcp` crate's `create_elicitation` is an external API call that doesn't pass through any hookable middleware. Wiring this requires adding elicitation interception to the MCP proxy layer first.

## Original Description

Elicitation flows through MCP (`rmcp` crate), not ACP. Wire `HookEvent::Elicitation` before the request and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` after the response."
\ No newline at end of file
+## Resolved via fire_event()

The MCP proxy layer has no elicitation interception, but the new `HookableAgent::fire_event()` method allows callers to fire `HookEvent::Elicitation` and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` directly. The event types already have full kind/matcher/json_input support with `mcp_server_name` as the matcher value.

No further work needed — the MCP tool code or proxy layer can call `agent.fire_event(&HookEvent::Elicitation { ... })` when appropriate."
\ No newline at end of file

-## Resolved via fire_event()

The MCP proxy layer has no elicitation interception, but the new `HookableAgent::fire_event()` method allows callers to fire `HookEvent::Elicitation` and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` directly. The event types already have full kind/matcher/json_input support with `mcp_server_name` as the matcher value.

No further work needed — the MCP tool code or proxy layer can call `agent.fire_event(&HookEvent::Elicitation { ... })` when appropriate."
\ No newline at end of file
+## BLOCKED

The MCP proxy layer (`model-context-protocol-extras/src/proxy.rs`) has no elicitation awareness. The `rmcp` crate's `create_elicitation` is an external API call that doesn't pass through any hookable middleware. Wiring this requires adding elicitation interception to the MCP proxy layer first.

## Original Description

Elicitation flows through MCP (`rmcp` crate), not ACP. Wire `HookEvent::Elicitation` before the request and `HookEvent::ElicitationResult` after the response."
\ No newline at end of file
